name: coding
description: Protein-coding exons. ~20000 protein-coding genes, ~180000 exons, ~1.5% of the genome. Average exon length 145 bp; mean coding-sequence length per gene ~1.4 kb. GC content 45-60% with strong variation: housekeeping genes and isochores H2/H3 are GC-rich, tissue-specific genes are GC-moderate. Codon usage is biased (e.g. CTG > TTA for Leu, GCC > GCA for Ala). orf_density must be high and codons must conform to the codon_table.json frequencies. Shannon 5-mer entropy is high (3.5-4.5) because triplet usage spreads probability across many 5-mers.
